title: Teen found dismembered in Chelsea parking lot is identified 26 years later
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Tiffany Bradley, a 16-year-old from Pennsylvania, was identified as the teenager found dismembered in Chelsea in 2000.
Eugene McCollom, who confessed to Bradley's murder, remains incarcerated after being charged shortly after the crime.
Authorities identified Bradley using genealogical DNA from her brother in Texas, highlighting advances in forensic technology.

title: Olney: Red Sox could take on a ‘bad contract’ in trade for right-handed bat
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox rank 27th in runs and 29th in home runs, struggling offensively at Fenway Park.
Red Sox are actively searching for a right-handed hitter, willing to take on a 'bad contract' in a trade.
Potential trade targets include Giants' Willy Adames, Giants' Matt Chapman, Mets' Bo Bichette, and Padres' Fernando Tatis Jr.

title: Here’s how to score one of the new commemorative CharlieCards
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: MBTA released special edition CharlieCards featuring Boston landmarks such as the Boston Public Library and USS Constitution.
The commemorative cards are available at Park Street, Downtown Crossing, and Forest Hills transit fare machines.
More than a dozen T stations will distribute the souvenir cards within the next week, availability is not guaranteed.

title: Jaylen Brown’s Fort Point penthouse is back on the market for $4.9m
author: Megan Johnson
bullets: Jaylen Brown's Fort Point penthouse re-listed at $4.9 million, now includes an adjacent studio residence.
The 3,500-square-foot condo features four bedrooms, industrial design, and a 400-square-foot roof deck.
Originally built in 1910, the property is listed by George Sarkis and Roy Bahnam of The Sarkis Team.

title: Patriots WR Kayshon Boutte ‘interested’ in a trade, NFL insider says
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Kayshon Boutte, Patriots wide receiver, is reportedly interested in a trade, according to ESPN's Adam Schefter.
Boutte missed the first two open OTA sessions and is entering the final year of his rookie contract.
Patriots' receiver room includes A.J. Brown, Romeo Doubs, Mack Hollins, possibly prompting wide receiver changes.

title: Visiting Boston this summer? Here are 8 navigation tips you need to know.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston expects millions of visitors this summer due to the FIFA World Cup and America’s 250th anniversary events.
Rideshare passengers at Logan Airport must use designated pickup areas; taxis are available at all terminals' arrival levels.
Visitors can use credit cards or the MBTA mTicket app for subway and commuter rail tickets without a CharlieCard.

title: Rent control proponents offer compromise with local option instead of statewide cap
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Rent control supporters propose a compromise allowing local municipalities in Massachusetts to opt into a modified rent control model.
The compromise limits rent hikes to the cost of living plus 5% with a 10% cap, allowing rent increases between tenancies.
The compromise faces opposition from some real estate groups, citing potential negative impacts on housing construction and consistency.

title: Report: Patriots ‘aware’ of A.J. Brown’s degenerative knee condition before trade, Eliot Wolf says
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots were aware of A.J. Brown's degenerative knee condition before trading for him, according to Eliot Wolf.
New England traded a 2028 first-round and a 2027 fifth-round pick to Philadelphia for A.J. Brown.
Brown assured reporters his knee is not a concern, despite other teams' hesitations due to his condition.

title: Report: Giannis Antetokounmpo is intrigued by idea of joining ‘wild card’ Celtics
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo is reportedly intrigued by the idea of joining the Boston Celtics, according to The Athletic's Sam Amick.
The Celtics, who won the NBA title in 2024, are viewed as a 'wild card' with Antetokounmpo focused on winning.
Bucks co-owner expects a decision on Antetokounmpo's future before the NBA Draft starting June 23rd.

title: E-bicyclist, 14, injured in collision with vehicle in Medway
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: A 14-year-old e-bicyclist was injured in a collision with a vehicle in Medway on Wednesday.
The accident occurred at the intersection of Village and Oakland Streets, officials reported.
Boston MedFlight transported the juvenile to a regional trauma center; injuries are not life-threatening.
